title: Computer aided design system for program-based representation of lattice structures
abstract: Methods and systems are disclosed for a computer aided design system that creates and analyzes a program-based representation of a complex lattice structure using a modeling engine, a synthesis engine and an analysis engine. The modeling engine generates a construct program that defines a lattice structure as a set of descriptive codes including an initial kernel, a starting point in space, and a series of transformations for a repetitive reproduction of the initial kernel into a virtual lattice structure. The modeling engine edits a construct program via a three-dimensional graphical user interface and hypertext editor. The synthesis engine performs design space exploration to generate an optimized design by optimization of the initial design via lazy evaluation queries to various analysis algorithms and sends the optimized design to the modeling engine for a next iteration of editing.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A computer aided design system for program-based representation of a lattice structure, comprising: a memory having a plurality of application modules stored thereon; and a processor for executing the application modules, the application modules comprising: a modeling engine configured to: generate an original construct program that defines a virtual lattice structure as a set of descriptive codes including an initial kernel, a starting point in space, and a series of transformations for a repetitive reproduction of the initial kernel into the virtual lattice structure; edit an existing construct program via a three-dimensional graphical user interface and hypertext editor; a synthesis engine configured to perform design space exploration to: generate an optimized design by optimization of the initial design via lazy evaluation queries to various analysis algorithms; and send the optimized design to the modeling engine for a next iteration of editing; and an analysis engine configured to: execute one or more query programs that analyze the virtual lattice structure in response to a received query from the modeling engine or the synthesis engine without a full geometric representation of the entire lattice structure being instantiated. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein the query programs include a geometric module configured to solve a query about a single point within external boundaries of the virtual lattice structure. 3. The system of claim 1 , wherein the solution to the single point query includes whether the single point is located on the virtual lattice structure. 4. The system of claim 1 , wherein the solution to the single point query includes a distance between the single point and one of the external boundaries of the virtual lattice structure. 5. The system of claim 1 , wherein the query programs include a material module configured to solve a query about a material-related property of the virtual lattice structure. 6. The system of claim 1 , wherein the query programs include a visualization module configured to render a portion of the virtual lattice structure. 7. The system of claim 1 , wherein the query programs include a mass property module configured to solve a query about integral properties of the virtual lattice structure. 8. The system of claim 1 , wherein the query programs include a multi- physics module configured to solve a query about predicting test results for simulation of one or more physical tests of the virtual lattice structure. 9. The system of claim 1 , wherein the query programs include a manufacturability module configured to solve a query about whether the current design is manufacturable for a given set of fabrication constraints. 10. The system of claim 1 , wherein the kernel of the virtual lattice is a pair of interlaced rings and the construct program represents a complex woven ring lattice structure in which the rings do not intersect. 1 1 . A computer-based method for designing a program-based representation of a lattice structure, comprising: generating an original construct program that defines a virtual lattice structure as a set of descriptive codes including an initial kernel, a starting point in space, and a series of transformations for a repetitive reproduction of the initial kernel into the virtual lattice structure; editing an existing construct program via a three-dimensional graphical user interface and hypertext editor; generating an optimized design by optimization of the initial design via lazy evaluation queries to various analysis algorithms; and executing one or more query programs that analyze the virtual lattice structure in response to a received query without a full geometric representation of the entire lattice structure being instantiated. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the query programs include a geometric module configured to solve a query about a single point within external boundaries of the virtual lattice structure. 13. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the solution to the single point query includes whether the single point is located on the virtual lattice structure. 14. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the solution to the single point query includes a distance between the single point and one of the external boundaries of the virtual lattice structure. 15. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the query programs include a material module configured to solve a query about a material-related property of the virtual lattice structure. 16. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the query programs include a visualization module configured to render a portion of the virtual lattice structure. 17. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the query programs include a mass property module configured to solve a query about integral properties of the virtual lattice structure. 18. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the query programs include a multi- physics module configured to solve a query about predicting test results for simulation of one or more physical tests of the virtual lattice structure. 19. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the query programs include a manufacturability module configured to solve a query about whether the current design is manufacturable for a given set of fabrication constraints. 20. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the kernel of the virtual lattice is a pair of interlaced rings and the construct program represents a complex woven ring lattice structure in which the rings do not intersect.
